title: Make a To-Do List
summary: Organize your tasks so you know what to do first.
learnerPromise: Organize your tasks so you know what to do first.
whyItMatters: Your brain is for thinking, not for storing tasks. Writing things down frees your mind and keeps you on track.
realLifeUses: List homework assignments for the day.
Plan errands you need to run.
Track tasks for a group project.
youWillLearn: Write down every task you need to do.
Break big tasks into smaller steps.
Review and update your list daily.
miniChallenge: Write down everything you need to do today, then pick the top 3.
steps: Write down every task on your mind. Do not worry about order yet.
Break large tasks into small, specific actions.
Pick your top three tasks for today.
Work through them one at a time.
Check off each item as you finish it and review what is left.
completionCriteria: You create a list with at least five tasks.
You check off at least three tasks by the end of the day.
commonProblems: Making tasks too vague or too big.
Creating a list that is unrealistically long.
Not looking at the list after writing it.
tips: Write tasks as actions: 'Call dentist' not 'Dentist.'
Limit daily tasks to a realistic number.
Keep your list where you will see it all day.
